title: Affordable Care Act (ACA)
summary: Expanded health insurance coverage through individual mandates, Medicaid expansion, and marketplace exchanges. Required coverage of pre-existing conditions.

title: SNAP (Food Stamps) Expansion
summary: Temporarily expanded SNAP eligibility and benefit amounts as part of the American Recovery and Reinvestment Act to address food insecurity during the recession.

title: Federal Minimum Wage Increase to $7.25
summary: Raised the federal minimum wage from $6.55 to $7.25 per hour, the third step of an increase signed into law in 2007.

title: FLSA Overtime Rule Update
summary: Proposed doubling the salary threshold for overtime exemption to $47,476, potentially extending overtime protections to 4.2 million workers.

title: Title IX Education Amendments
summary: Prohibits sex-based discrimination in any education program or activity receiving federal financial assistance, spurring expansion of women's sports programs.

title: Clean Air Act Amendments
summary: Strengthened regulation of air pollutants, established cap-and-trade for sulfur dioxide, and tightened vehicle emission standards.

title: CARES Act — COVID-19 Relief
summary: Provided $2.2 trillion in economic stimulus including $1,200 direct payments, expanded unemployment benefits, and $350B in small business loans.

title: Student Loan Debt Relief Plan
summary: Proposed up to $10,000 in federal student loan forgiveness ($20,000 for Pell Grant recipients) for borrowers under $125,000 annual income.

title: Earned Income Tax Credit Expansion
summary: Expanded EITC for childless workers, nearly tripling the maximum credit and raising the income cap as part of the American Rescue Plan.

title: Expanded Child Tax Credit
summary: Temporarily expanded the Child Tax Credit to $3,000–$3,600 per child with monthly advance payments, reducing child poverty by an estimated 40%.

title: Federal Emergency Paid Leave (FFCRA)
summary: Required employers with fewer than 500 employees to provide up to 80 hours of paid sick leave and 12 weeks of expanded family and medical leave for COVID-19 reasons.

title: CDC Eviction Moratorium
summary: Halted residential evictions for non-payment of rent for tenants meeting income thresholds, protecting an estimated 40 million renters during the pandemic.

title: Medicare Drug Price Negotiation
summary: Allowed Medicare to negotiate prices for up to 10 high-cost drugs by 2026, expanding to 20+ by 2029, capping out-of-pocket drug costs at $2,000/year.

title: Bipartisan Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act
summary: Invested $1.2 trillion in physical infrastructure including $550B in new spending on roads, bridges, rail, broadband, and clean water systems.

title: Voting Rights Act
summary: Prohibited discriminatory voting practices that had disenfranchised Black Americans, requiring federal approval for changes to voting laws in covered jurisdictions.

title: Deferred Action for Childhood Arrivals (DACA)
summary: Provided renewable two-year periods of deferred action from deportation and eligibility for work permits to individuals brought to the US as children.

title: CHIPS and Science Act
summary: Provided $52.7B for domestic semiconductor manufacturing and research, and $200B for scientific R&D to boost US competitiveness with China.

title: Clean Power Plan
summary: Required US power sector to cut carbon dioxide emissions 32% below 2005 levels by 2030, promoting shift from coal to natural gas and renewables.

title: OSHA Heat Illness Prevention Standard (Proposed)
summary: Proposed requiring employers to provide water, shade, and rest breaks to outdoor and indoor workers when heat reaches 80°F, with enhanced protections above 90°F.

title: Housing First — Chronic Homelessness Initiative
summary: Prioritized moving chronically homeless individuals directly into permanent housing before addressing other issues, reversing the traditional treatment-first ladder approach.
